items.title: Investing the Buffett Way: Lessons for Today's Investors
items.description: We discuss what Buffett's retirement means for Berkshire and investors.

items.title: Nvidia Termed 'Undervalued' By Masayoshi Son Last Year, Now Worth $5 Trillion: Here Is What Japanese Billionaire Investor Said About Ongoing AI Hype
items.description: SoftBank CEO Masayoshi Son's 2024 prediction that Nvidia was "undervalued" has proven accurate as Jensen Huang's chipmaking giant surpasses a $5 trillion market cap, validating Son's bold $9 trillion AI vision.

items.title: Apple Expecting Strong Holiday Season
items.description: Apple is set to kick off its 50th year with what's expected to be a nearly $140 billion quarter. Bloomberg's Mark Gurman discusses with Caroline Hyde on "Bloomberg Tech."

items.title: The AI Trade Is Still Strong. Investors Have to Go Along for the Ride.
items.description: Investors that want to beat the S&P 500 likely have to stay invested in big tech while looking to make outsize bets elsewhere.

items.title: The Magnificent 7's biggest commonality is growth, Jim Cramer says
items.description: CNBC's Jim Cramer on Monday rejected concerns that market cap is too concentrated in the Magnificent Seven.
